Support GARI
Accessibility research. Practical technology. Real lives.

Building tools that help people live with more independence.

The Global Accessibility Research Institute is a nonprofit organization developing practical assistive technology, education tools, and accessibility research programs for families and communities facing real-world challenges.

Explore Our Work Help Fund the Mission
Nonprofit MissionBuilt around charitable, educational, and research purposes.
Practical PrototypesFocused on tools families can actually use.
Human-CenteredDesigned around stress, access, learning, and dignity.
First initiative: Insulin Buddy
Technology should make hard moments easier.

Insulin Buddy Station
A family-centered support station for newly diagnosed pediatric diabetes families.

Future accessibility research
Speech, vision, education, navigation, and assistive technology initiatives.
Our mission

GARI exists to turn accessibility ideas into usable systems.

We focus on the gap between a real human need and a tool that can reduce stress, improve access, and help people move through life with greater confidence.

✓
Assistive technology development
Designing practical tools that support families, children, caregivers, and people with disabilities.
✓
Accessibility research
Exploring how technology, education, and systems engineering can improve independence and inclusion.
✓
Education and support
Creating clear learning resources that help people understand and manage complex daily challenges.
✓
Prototype-to-impact pathway
Building, testing, improving, and deploying tools with real-world feedback.
Programs and initiatives

Insulin Buddy is the first project. GARI is the larger mission.

GARI is designed as an umbrella organization for multiple accessibility and assistive technology initiatives.

Active prototype initiative

Insulin Buddy

A practical family-centered station designed to help newly diagnosed pediatric diabetes families organize education, supplies, reminders, and daily management routines after leaving the hospital.

Visit Insulin Buddy
EchoReader

Future literacy and speech-support tools that help children read aloud with feedback and encouragement.

AURA Accessibility

Future navigation and independence concepts using phones, sensors, audio, and assistive interfaces.

Accessibility Research Lab

Research, prototypes, and community partnerships focused on practical accessibility problems.

What makes us different

We are focused on usable, fundable, testable solutions.

GARI is not trying to chase technology for its own sake. We want tools that can be explained clearly, built responsibly, tested honestly, and improved with feedback.

⚙️

Systems thinking

We look at the whole problem: the user, the workflow, the environment, the training burden, and the risk of failure.

🤝

Community feedback

Families, educators, clinicians, and accessibility stakeholders should shape how tools are tested and improved.

🧭

Mission discipline

We prioritize projects that connect directly to independence, education, organization, safety, and dignity.

How support helps

Donations move ideas from concept to prototype to families.

Early support helps GARI build credible prototypes, create educational materials, gather feedback, and prepare pilot deployments for real users.

Support the Mission
1
Prototype development
Hardware, software, design, and user workflow development.
2
Educational content
Plain-language materials, videos, guides, and support resources.
3
Pilot deployments
Testing with families, educators, community partners, and professional advisors.

Founder perspective

Built with engineering discipline and family-centered urgency.

GARI was founded to connect technical skill with real human problems. The goal is to build practical tools that reduce stress, improve organization, and help people navigate difficult life challenges with better support.

This section can later include a stronger founder story, professional background, why accessibility matters personally, and why GARI is starting with Insulin Buddy.

Support GARI

Help us build practical accessibility technology.

We are building the foundation for a nonprofit research institute that can prototype, test, and deploy tools for families and communities who need practical support.
